rationale: Both correctly identify this as a mechanically consistent rename with all call sites updated and tests aligned, reaching the same APPROVE verdict as the ground truth's terse 'lgtm' — recall is trivially satisfied for both since ground truth surfaces no substantive concerns. X shows deeper root-cause understanding (explains the AttributeError → 500 mechanism via upstream's base() helper) and cites concrete file:line locations for its verification, giving it an edge in actionable insight. Y is thorough and honestly flags its 'serving_tokens naming' claim as an unverified assumption rather than asserting it as fact, but its findings read more as a mechanical checklist without explaining why the bug mattered, and its raw workflow-report format is less digestible/actionable than X's structured review.
